title: -Appeal of Special Assessment Tax Lien and of the Need for Abatement Action at 2750 Cedar Street

sections.recommendation:
Conduct a public hearing and upon conclusion adopt a Resolution denying the property
owners’ appeal of a special assessment tax lien and of the necessity and cost of City
abatement actions and allowing the special assessment lien for $24,831.37 for 2750
Cedar Street (APN 058 221102001) to be recorded with the County of Alameda.
SUMMARY
This report is in response to an appeal of an impending special assessment tax lien and
of the necessity and cost of summary abatement actions taken by the City at 2750
Cedar Street in October 2024. The lien reflects costs incurred by the City to abate
unpermitted and unsafe construction that continued despite repeated notices and
orders, Stop Work Orders, and administrative citations.
City staff documented ongoing demolition of a dilapidated existing structure and new
construction performed without the required City permits or inspections, including
continued construction work conducted in violation of six Stop Work Orders and two
citations. Due to the hazardous conditions created by the partially demolished existing
structure and the construction of an unpermitted structure—particularly given the site’s
steep slope, location in a California Geological Survey Landslide Zone and in a High
Fire Hazard Severity Zone, a failing retaining wall, close proximity to neighboring
properties, and limited fire access—the City obtained an administrative warrant and
performed a lawful summary abatement to eliminate the hazards.
The property owners, through their architect, Michael Tolleson, appealed the proposed
lien and the need for and cost of abatement in a timely manner. The issues raised by
the owner in the appeal are addressed in this report. Based on the administrative
record, staff concludes that the abatement action was necessary and properly
authorized, and that the costs reflected in the lien are accurate and recoverable under
applicable law.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510)
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The lien of $24,831.37 will allow the City to recover its costs from the abatement of
unpermitted construction at 2750 Cedar Street.
In addition, administrative citations of $18,000 were issued and remain unpaid. Those
citations are not included in the amount proposed in the lien.
sections.current_situation:
On November 13, 2025, the Building and Safety Division notified the legal property
owners, Paul T Mak and Ping H Mak, that a lien would be placed for the unpaid balance
of $24,831.37 owed for the abatement of unpermitted construction at 2750 Cedar
Street. On November 19, 2025, Michael Tolleson, Architect, acting on behalf of the
owners, submitted a timely objection to the proposed lien. On December 10, 2025, Mr.
Tolleson submitted an additional “basis for appeal” letter. Due to the hazardous
condition of the property, the City proceeded with the summary abatement. The
appellant’s prior appeal of the need for the abatement action is being heard together
with their appeal of the lien. Collectively these appeal documents are included with this
report as Attachment 2. This Council hearing is required to decide both aspects of the
appeal, the lien and the need for the abatement actions.
sections.background:
The Berkeley Municipal Code (BMC) establishes Front, Rear, and Side Yards for main buildings in
Residential Zones based on the locations of the corresponding lot lines. As described below, in the
case of an irregularly-shaped lot, the Zoning Officer must determine the front of the lot for purposes
of lot frontage and required yards, and designate the required yards.
The subject lot at 2750 Cedar Street is located in the Single-Family Residential District and Hillside
Overlay (R-1H), and has frontages on Le Vereda Road and Cedar Street. These street lot lines are
separated by a corner lot at 1601 Le Vereda Road. The lot itself is also an irregular shape, with
narrow flag portions and a total of 10 lot lines. (See Images #1 and #2)

Per BMC 23E.96. 070.C for the Hillside Overlay:
Main buildings shall be set back from the respective lot lines, and separated between one another,
as required by the regulations for the underlying District which is combined with the H District, except
that such setbacks and building separations may be reduced subject to obtaining an AUP and
making the required finding under Section 23E.96.090.B. In addition, yards and building separation
shall also be subject to the limitation and exception provisions set forth in
Sections 23D.04.030 or 23E.04.030, as the case may be.

sections.rationale:
The issues raised in the appellant’s letters (Attachment 2) and staff’s responses are
detailed below. For the sake of brevity, the appeal issues are not re-stated in their
entirety.
ISSUE 1: The property at 2750 Cedar consisted of a permitted and approved 725-
square-foot, one-story single-family residence that was 50% demolished as of 2015.
The intent of the Architect was to reconstruct the residence to match the original
structure and to add an ADU and associated site work in phases and to submit a Zoning
Use Permit for the full project. The applicant maintains that reconstruction of the original
single-family residence, identical to the pre-existing structure, should be permitted by
right.
RESPONSE 1: While the property contained a previously permitted single-family
residence, the original structure was previously partially demolished (with a permit) in
2013. The remaining structure was not properly maintained, nor was a building permit to
rebuild ever issued. During the recent demolition of the remaining structure the
Page 7
Page 8 of 785
Appeal of Special Assessment Tax Lien and of the Need for PUBLIC HEARING
Abatement Action at 2750 Cedar St March 10, 2026
demolition was no longer considered partial, per the definition of Demolition in BMC
23.502.020(D)(2), and any new project being considered on the site would require a
Use Permit, per BMC 23.202.020 Table 23.202-1. Any subsequent construction
constitutes new work subject to current permitting, plan review, and code requirements.
An intent to entirely build a structure “virtually identical” to the original structure does not
confer by-right approval; there is no such provision in the building standards code. The
project is for a new structure and must comply with all applicable City regulations and
permit requirements in effect at the time of a completed building permit application.
Land Use clearly informed Mr. Tolleson of the Use Permit requirement.
The Land Use Division issued a correction during the permi

Application #: B2024-02077 June 27, 2024
Project Address: 2750 Cedar St.
Dear Project Applicant,
The City of Berkeley has completed review of the construction documents submitted under the
building permit application number specified above. This letter includes comments and
corrections from all City of Berkeley reviewing agencies, which must be addressed in order to
obtain the permits for the proposed work indicated in the construction documents.
Plans revised in response to corrections must be accompanied with a written response letter,
which explicitly addresses each correction and the corresponding plan sheet number, revision
number and date. A full plan set of ALL construction drawings is required when responding to
corrections. Incomplete or unclear responses to corrections may result in delays.
Document formatting requirements:
• All permit documents must be submitted in electronic format, as unsecured PDF files.
• Documents with multiple pages must be combined and named according to content.
• Documents that are incomplete or improperly formatted will not be processed.
• Code Enforcement cases must be indicated by case number. Dates specified in
Notice(s) of Violation take precedence over any timelines specified in this document.
